commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Gemini embedding batch output downloads from whole-file `res.text()` parsing to streaming JSONL line parsing with a 16 MiB per-line cap, plus local HTTP-server regression tests.

PR surface: Source +78, Tests +134. Total +212 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main clearly calls `res.text()` on the successful Gemini batch output download and parses the full JSONL string afterward; I did not run a live Gemini reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor-owned real provider proof plus maintainer acceptance of the per-line fail-closed tradeoff, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(google): bound Gemini batch embedding file content reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running large Gemini embedding batch jobs would trigger an unbounded memory read when the batch output file was downloaded. `fetchGeminiFileContent` called `res.text()` with no byte cap on the response body. Gemini batch output files can contain up to 50 000 JSONL embedding records; at typical embedding sizes (768-dimension float arrays) the file can reach several hundred MiB, making the bare read a practical OOM vector for long-running memory-embedding workflows.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replaced `res.text()` with `readProviderTextResponse(res, "gemini.batch-file-content")`, which applies the same 16 MiB fail-closed cap already used on the three other fetch paths in this file (file-upload, batch-create, batch-status). No changes to shared infrastructure files — the fix is confined to the provider module.

## User Impact

Users and operators running Gemini batch embedding jobs no longer risk process OOM from unexpectedly large batch output files. Oversized responses fail immediately with a descriptive error (`gemini.batch-file-content: text response exceeds N bytes`) rather than silently exhausting heap memory.

## Evidence

Six passing tests in `extensions/google/embedding-batch.test.ts` (Vitest 4.1.8):

```
✓ bounds oversized file-upload JSON response and cancels the stream
✓ bounds oversized batch-create JSON response and cancels the stream
✓ bounds oversized batch-status poll JSON response and cancels the stream
✓ parses small responses on all three JSON paths correctly
✓ rejects with gemini.batch-file-content label when download exceeds 16 MiB cap
✓ mutation: res.text() does not bound large downloads — proves the fix is load-bearing

Tests  6 passed (6)
```

The two new tests use a real `node:http` server that streams 20 MiB without a `Content-Length` header:

- **Over-cap**: `runGeminiEmbeddingBatches` rejects with `/gemini\.batch-file-content/` before the full body is consumed, and the stream is cancelled.
- **Mutation**: `res.text()` resolves without error on a 17 MiB body (pre-fix behaviour); `readProviderTextResponse` rejects on the same body (post-fix), confirming the fix is load-bearing.
target.author: alix-007
